lessons.text: Face danger without fear, and keep your heart clean.
Master your impulses; give without holding back.
Tell the truth. Harm nothing.
Let anger go. Stay gentle, want less.
Forgive quickly. Carry no grudge, no pride.
Good character frees you; cruelty traps you.
Know what to do, and what to leave alone.
Don't treat the world as meaningless.
Drop the pretense and the endless chasing.
Your possessions aren't proof of your worth.
Don't let fantasy drag you under.
Don't make a show of your good acts.
Arrogance curdles into cruelty. Drop it.
Shut the three gates of ruin: lust, anger, greed.
Free of those, do what truly serves you.
Don't let impulse override hard-won wisdom.
Let sound principles decide your actions.
You become what you believe.
Don't punish yourself to prove your discipline.
Eat what brings you health, strength, and calm.
Skip food that overstimulates and leaves you wrecked.
Refuse what's stale, spoiled, and lifeless.
Do good because it's right, not for reward.
Good done for reward and display is hollow.
Respect what's wise; keep yourself clean and gentle.
Speak what's true, kind, and calm.
Steady your mind with calm and self-control.
Discipline done for applause won't last.
Never turn discipline into self-harm or cruelty.
Give to those who need it, expecting nothing back.
Give generously, never grudgingly or for payback.
Give with respect, at the right time and place.
Without conviction, your effort comes to nothing.
Give up the craving, not the work.
Don't abandon the practices that better you.
Even good work, do it without clinging to results.
Never quit your duty out of confusion.
Don't quit hard work just to dodge discomfort.
Do what's needed simply because it's right.
Don't dread hard tasks or crave easy ones.
You can't stop acting, so release the results.
Cling to outcomes and they own you.
Many forces shape any outcome, not just you.
Stop taking sole credit for what happens.
Act without ego, and nothing binds you.
See the same thread running through everyone.
Don't mistake one piece for the whole picture.
Do your duty without craving, favoritism, or spite.
Weigh the cost and your limits before acting.
Work with resolve, unmoved by win or loss.
Root out laziness, deceit, and delay.
Know clearly what to pursue and what to drop.
Don't let confusion make wrong look right.
Steady your mind, your breath, your senses.
Stop clinging to fear, grief, and laziness.
Choose the joy that's hard first, sweet later.
Beware pleasures sweet now, bitter later.
Let your natural strengths shape your work.
You grow most by mastering your own work.
Pour your whole heart into ordinary work.
Better your own path done badly than another's done well.
Every job has flaws; don't quit yours over them.
Master your wants, and action stops weighing on you.
Clear your head; let go of craving and hate.
Live simply, speak less, sit with yourself.
Let go of ego, arrogance, and 'mine.'
Grow calm, and treat everyone as equal.
Devote yourself fully, and real understanding follows.
Do everything, but stay anchored to something steady.
Act fully, then let go of what you did.
Drop the ego, and the obstacles shrink.
Your nature will act, whether your ego likes it or not.

lessons.note: Pure metaphysics (a higher power dwelling in every heart, turning all beings) — no standalone modern kernel distinct from the surrender lines. Drop.

lessons.text: Give yourself fully, and peace follows.
Think it through, then decide for yourself.
Fix your focus, and you'll get there.
Do your best, then stop clinging to the rest.
Don't force advice on those who won't hear it.

lessons.note: Ritual: studying this text framed as an act of worship — self-referential to scripture, no universal kernel beyond the 'listen with focus' lines. Drop.

lessons.text: Listen openly, without a hostile heart.
Listen with full attention, and confusion clears.
Let clarity return, then act without hesitation.
